name: Route of the Madalena River - circular from Vilalba
name_original: Ruta del río Madalena – circular desde Vilalba
description: This easy 2.8-mile (4.5 km) jogging route along the Madalena River in Vilalba combines natural beauty with a unique literary path.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Vilalba are found within the Terra Chá region of Galicia, Spain, characterized by its relatively flat terrain and extensive green spaces. The area is shaped by the Madalena River, offering accessible river walks and parks. Vilalba is also part of the Terras do Miño Biosphere Reserve, providing a broader landscape of rivers, streams, and ancient stone bridges. These features create varied environments for running, from well-maintained paths to more natural river trails. Best jogging routes around Vilalba The most popular jogging route is Bidueiras Trail, a 6.8 miles (11.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a good balance of distance and elevation gain. Another top favourite among local runners is Route of the Madalena River - circular from Vilalba, an easy 2.8 miles (4.5 km) path. This route follows the scenic Madalena River, featuring parks and wooden bridges. Local runners also love the Andrade Tower – Cemetery with a small chapel loop from Cemiterio de Insua, a 20.6 miles (33.2 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 40 minutes. Jogging around Vilalba is defined by river walks, green spaces, and sections of the Camino del Norte, offering routes for different ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 60 runners have used komoot to explore Vilalba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Vilalba?
A: There are over 10 dedicated running routes around Vilalba, catering to various fitness levels. These trails explore the scenic landscapes of the Terra Chá region, offering diverse experiences from river walks to more challenging paths.

Q: Are there easy jogging paths suitable for beginners or families in Vilalba?
A: Yes, Vilalba offers several easy jogging paths. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991745976/route-of-the-madalena-river-circular-from-vilalba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Madalena River - circular from Vilalba&lt;/a&gt;. This 2.8-mile (4.5 km) route follows the scenic Madalena River, featuring parks and charming wooden bridges, making it ideal for a relaxed run or a family outing.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Vilalba?
A: The terrain around Vilalba is generally characterized by the relatively flat landscape of the Terra Chá region. You'll find well-maintained river walks, green spaces, and sections that are part of the Camino del Norte. While many routes are accessible, some trails, particularly those extending into the Terras do Miño Biosphere Reserve, may include varied natural landscapes and gentle inclines.

Q: Are there any long-distance or more challenging running routes in the area?
A: For those seeking a longer or more challenging run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38174414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Andrade Tower – Cemetery with a small chapel loop from Cemiterio de Insua&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 20.6-mile (33.2 km) trail. It leads through varied landscapes and is often completed in about 3 hours 40 minutes, offering a significant endurance test.

Q: Can I find circular running routes around Vilalba?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Vilalba are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31878247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mámoa de Camplelos 2 loop from Begonte&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.8-mile (7.7 km) circular trail that offers a pleasant experience.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while jogging in Vilalba?
A: Jogging in Vilalba offers views of the Madalena River with its picturesque literary and river walk, traditional small mills, and wooded islands. You might also encounter ancient stone bridges and sections of the Camino del Norte. Notable landmarks in the broader area include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/363288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Andrade Tower&lt;/a&gt; and the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/359319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the running trails in Vilalba dog-friendly?
A: While specific regulations for each trail may vary, the natural and open character of Vilalba's running routes, especially along the Madalena River and within the Terras do Miño Biosphere Reserve, generally makes them suitable for running with dogs. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash and clean up after them to preserve the natural environment.

Q: Is there public transport access to the jogging routes in Vilalba?
A: Vilalba is a town with local public transport options that can provide access to various starting points for running routes. As a significant point on the Camino del Norte, it also has connections that might facilitate reaching certain trailheads. For specific route access, checking local bus schedules or taxi services would be advisable.

Q: Where can I find parking near the running trails in Vilalba?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Vilalba, especially near popular recreational areas like the Madalena River. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40307234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Vilalba&lt;/a&gt;, start directly from the town, making it convenient to find parking in municipal areas or designated spots close to the trailheads.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Vilalba?
A: Over 60 runners have explored Vilalba's varied terrain using komoot. They often praise the region's accessible and enjoyable routes, particularly the well-maintained river walks and the peaceful green spaces. The mix of natural beauty and cultural elements, like the 'Paseo dos Soños' with its sculptures, also adds to the unique running experience.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or points of interest along the Madalena River walk?
A: Yes, the Recreational Area of Madalena and its River Walk is a highlight. Along the 'Paseo dos Soños' (Path of Dreams), you can enjoy a unique cultural experience with sculptures and texts dedicated to local writers integrated into the natural surroundings every 100 meters. The path also features charming wooden bridges, traditional small mills, and a river beach with a picnic area.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Vilalba?
A: Vilalba, located in Galicia, Spain, experiences a mild climate. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for running, with lush green landscapes. Summer can be warm but is still enjoyable, especially for early morning or late afternoon runs. Winter runs are also possible, though you might encounter cooler, wetter conditions.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty running routes in Vilalba?
A: Yes, Vilalba offers several moderate running routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991256877/bidueiras-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bidueiras Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.8 miles (11.0 km) long and takes approximately 1 hour 23 minutes to complete. This route provides a good balance of distance and elevation, suitable for runners looking for a bit more challenge than an easy path.
tips: Vilalba is the capital of the Terra Chá region, in the province of Lugo. It is a quiet town where the tower of the homage of the Castillo de los Andrade and the church of Santa María, in neoclassical style, stand out.

At the exit of the town of Vilalba is the fluvial beach.
Although the water of the Magdalena River is quite cold, a cap on very hot days can refresh us. The beach is located in a recreational area with pools, slides, tables and benches in the shade.

This tower with an octagonal base, an old homage tower, is the only vestige that reminds us of the existence of a fortress castle, whose origins date back to the 11th century, although this element dates from the 15th. Its silhouette dominates Vilalba, capital of the region known as Terra Chá. It is known that the old castle, successively remodeled from its origin, already had three towers and a polygonal wall in the 13th century. It belonged to the lords of Lemos, but later King Pedro I donated it to Fernán Pérez de Andrade or Boo, thus passing it to the heritage of this noble family, which will leave its heraldic symbols in the construction, such as the stone representation of the boar .

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Route of the Madalena River?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 4.5 kilometers long and generally takes about 29 minutes to jog, depending on your pace.

faqs.question: Is the Route of the Madalena River suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is considered an "easy run" with low difficulty, making it suitable for all fitness levels, including beginners. It features minimal elevation gain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The path is predominantly paved and well-maintained, offering a smooth surface for jogging. There are slight ascents in some sections, but overall it's an accessible and pleasant riverside trail.

faqs.question: What notable sights will I encounter along the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll experience typical riverside landscapes, lush vegetation, and the tranquil Madalena River. Highlights include small dams and historical mills like Os Pasos and Madalena, charming wooden bridges crossing the river, and a small island with various tree species. Don't miss the Church of Santa María de Vilalba, the Vilalba Riverside Beach, and the Andrade Tower.

faqs.question: Is the Route of the Madalena River dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally welcoming for dogs, but it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in recreational areas and near other visitors, to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant conditions with mild temperatures and beautiful natural scenery. Summer is also great for enjoying the riverside amenities.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and enjoy the Route of the Madalena River.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Route of the Madalena River?
faqs.answer: As the route is circular from Vilalba, you can typically find parking within the town. The trail leads to a well-equipped recreational area which often has convenient parking options nearby.

faqs.question: Are there facilities or amenities available along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route leads to a fluvial beach and a well-equipped recreational area. Here you'll find a fountain, a spacious picnic spot, restrooms, and a bar-cafeteria, perfect for a break or refreshments.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails, including the Northern Route (Galiza), Mtb Northern Route (Galiza), Travesia Pedreira Segunda, Travesía do Matadoiro, and Camiño Pasos.

faqs.question: What makes this route unique?
faqs.answer: Beyond its natural beauty, the route features the "Paseo dos Soños" (Path of Dreams), a literary path adorned with original sculptures every hundred meters and literary "hectometers" dedicated to local writers and artists. This adds a rich cultural dimension to your outdoor activity.

waypoints.name: Church of Santa María de Vilalba
